authoritative_sources.title: Cosmetics Labeling Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA is the primary U.S. cosmetics regulator. Its Cosmetics Labeling Guide is the direct public operating reference for beauty-retail label handling, including off-package ingredient labeling, display-unit leaflets, direct-mail notices, and shade-line declarations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Beauty product setup, display, and label-evidence validation

authoritative_sources.title: Summary of Cosmetics Labeling Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's summary page is the authoritative public baseline for product identity, net contents, distributor identity, ingredient declarations, and misbranding posture, which directly affects beauty retail evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Label sufficiency and product-master evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Is It a Cosmetic, a Drug, or Both? (Or Is It Soap?)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's classification guidance is the authoritative public source for when a personal-care item remains a cosmetic, becomes an OTC drug, or falls outside cosmetic scope, which is central to safe boundary routing for a beauty retail lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Product classification and adjacent-authority boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Do I need to label my cosmetics products with expiration dates?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's FAQ is the authoritative public statement that U.S. law does not generally require cosmetic expiration dates, while product safety substantiation still matters, which prevents the lane from inventing universal dating rules.
used_for: nonblocking ambiguity handling around expiration dates

authoritative_sources.title: Questions and Answers Regarding Mandatory Cosmetics Recalls
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's cosmetics-recall guidance is the current authoritative public reference for mandatory recall posture, responsible-person obligations, and safety-triggered escalation boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Business Guide to the FTC's Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary federal consumer-protection authority for retail order commitments. This guide is the direct public workflow reference for shipment timing, delay notices, cancellations, and refund posture in omnichannel beauty commerce.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Omnichannel order, pickup, ship-delay, and refund handling
used_for: completion evidence requirements for delayed orders

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising FAQ's: A Guide for Small Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC's advertising FAQ is the official public operating reference that sale, price-comparison, and other marketing claims must be truthful and substantiated, which directly constrains beauty promo and price-signage workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Pricing, sale-claim, and promo-signage control review

authoritative_sources.title: NIST HB 130-2025: Uniform Laws and Regulations in the Areas of Legal Metrology and Fuel Quality
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Office of Weights and Measures publishes the public U.S. baseline used by jurisdictions for retail price verification. Handbook 130 provides the operating procedure for comparing displayed and point-of-sale prices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Shelf, display, and POS price-verification handling
used_for: retail evidence capture for price mismatches

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for retail identifiers and barcode data. The General Specifications are the vendor-neutral reference for GTIN, data carriers, and barcode practices used across beauty retail systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Item identity, barcode, and system-linkage handling
used_for: item-record linkage across POS, OMS, and PIM

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Data Model Attribute Implementation Guideline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1's Global Data Model guideline is the vendor-neutral workflow reference for beauty and personal care attributes, data pools, and SPF-linked content handling, which makes it directly relevant to retail beauty master-data execution.

workflow_stages.name: Beauty assortment and product-class grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the item family, brand, shade, assortment context, product class, and whether the workflow stays inside ordinary beauty retail or crosses into OTC-drug, regulatory, or licensed-advice handling.

workflow_stages.name: Display, tester, and labeling prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify ingredient declarations, Drug Facts where applicable, warning text, display-unit leaflets, shade charts, planogram instructions, and other beauty-specific artifacts before execution progresses.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Pricing, promotion, and order execution
workflow_stages.description: Track advertised price, sale claims, order or pickup timing, POS behavior, and promo setup while preserving FTC and NIST evidence expectations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Returns, customer-impact, and franchise exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Package used-product returns, promo misses, and customer-impact exceptions without inventing price overrides, regulatory interpretations, or unsupported concessions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Recall, stop-sale, and safety-boundary control
workflow_stages.description: Preserve FDA-driven recall and safety boundaries when a beauty item becomes a stop-sale, recall, or adverse-event concern, especially when lot or batch evidence is incomplete.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and boundary-safe closure
workflow_stages.description: Close work only when product, price, order, display, and disposition records align across the designated systems of record and no open safety or compliance exception remains unresolved.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: product master records with SKU, GTIN, UPC, shade, assortment, and brand attributes
ingredient declarations, Drug Facts where applicable, warning statements, net contents, and distributor identity records
display charts, ingredient leaflets, tester-unit artifacts, planograms, promo calendars, and shelf or display evidence
POS receipts, OMS records, pickup or shipment status, price files, and CRM or loyalty notes
return, disposition, franchise-exception, recall, stop-sale, adverse-event, and incident records
lot or batch references, replenishment records, inventory states, and completion checklists
systems_of_record: document repositories for label and display artifacts

allowed: classify retail beauty work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, rework, on hold, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, approved pricing and return-policy records, product-label artifacts, and systems-of-record context needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed workflow summaries, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
escalate: questions about therapeutic claims, skin-safety interpretation, or whether a product should be used to diagnose, treat, mitigate, or prevent disease
final regulatory, legal, or compliance decisions about cosmetic versus OTC drug classification, label permissibility, or market release
final price-override approval, franchise concession approval, recall classification, or public-safety signoff
product-safety, adverse-event, pharmacist, or licensed esthetics decisions
refuse: advance or complete beauty work when required product, pricing, display, order, or closure evidence is missing and would change the outcome
treat the beauty label as cosmetic and answer with generic retail assumptions that ignore shade, ingredient, tester, or Drug Facts artifacts
bypass recall, stop-sale, or label-evidence controls to preserve promo timing, schedule adherence, or customer convenience

domain_failure_modes: a tester or display unit is put into service without the required ingredient leaflets, display chart, or product-identification artifacts
a shelf or sale sign is treated as sufficient even though POS or promo-table records disagree on the actual price
a beauty product with therapeutic or SPF positioning is handled as generic cosmetics work even though OTC-drug or compliance boundaries apply
used-product returns, customer-impact complaints, or franchise exceptions are resolved without the tenant policy or evidence that materially changes the outcome
a stop-sale or recall case is closed without tying affected lot or batch evidence to shelf, backroom, and open-order records
completion is declared without authoritative POS, OMS, display, or disposition evidence
the lane invents universal expiration-date or label rules for all beauty items instead of using product-class and tenant-specific controls

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State and local pricing, returns, and product-access rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Federal and NIST sources define the public baseline, but state or local consumer-protection rules and tenant franchise policy can materially narrow beauty-retail handling for sale pricing, used-product returns, or limited-quantity promotions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Assortment mix across cosmetics, OTC drugs, and non-cosmetic personal-care goods
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define the classification boundaries, but the tenant must supply which beauty categories it actually sells so the lane can route sunscreen, acne, dandruff, or other drug-adjacent items correctly at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tester hygiene and used-product disposition rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources constrain label and safety boundaries, but exact tester handling, used-cosmetics returns, and quarantine or destruction workflows are tenant-specific and must be retrieved rather than guessed.

notes: Authoritative records for this lane are the tenant-designated product-master, pricing, order, inventory, recall, label, and completion systems constrained by FDA, FTC, NIST, and GS1 public baselines.

The lane stops before regulatory interpretation, therapeutic guidance, final pricing approval, product-safety adjudication, and recall authority even when launch pressure or customer-impact pressure is high.
